title: malus sylvestris var. lemoinei
intro: Malus sylvestris var. lemoinei, also known as Lemoine's crabapple, is a variant of the wild apple tree (Malus sylvestris), a species of flowering plant in the rose family. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Malus sylvestris var. lemoinei include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
